title: OfficeCLI: Office suite for AI agents to read and edit Microsoft Office files
summary: OfficeCLI is an open-source command-line tool that lets AI agents read, edit, and automate Word, Excel, and PowerPoint documents without requiring a Microsoft Office installation, shipping as a single self-contained binary. It targets a persistent gap in agent workflows: Office file formats are widely used but awkward for LLM-driven tools to manipulate reliably, and a CLI interface fits naturally

title: Agentic coding notes
summary: A developer shares practical field notes from working with agentic coding tools, covering how autonomous coding agents behave in real projects and where they need guardrails or human oversight. It is useful reading for anyone integrating LLM-driven agents into a development workflow, since it reflects observed behavior rather than vendor claims.

title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven't progressed enough
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg reportedly told employees at an internal meeting that the company's AI agent work is advancing more slowly than expected, a notable admission given Meta's heavy investment in the space. For teams building agentic systems, it is a useful data point that even frontier labs are finding reliable, autonomous agents harder to ship than roadmaps suggested.
